title: 1. Spread Out
keyPhrase: Make the field wider when we have the ball
summaryBullets: Wide players stretch the field side to side.
A high central player stretches the defense front to back.
Support behind the ball keeps the team connected and safe.

scenarios.title: Crowded to Spread
scenarios.description: Don't crowd the field.  Move into space.  Create space on the field

frames.label: Crowded shape
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

frames.label: Opposite side play shift wider
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: Players on the other side need to be in space for the 2nd or 3rd pass.

frames.label: Better spacing
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: This creates space in the vertical-middle of the field

title: 2. We Move Together
summaryBullets: Teammates help most when they create visible passing lanes.
Support should give the ball carrier forward, sideways, and backward choices.
Players standing in straight lines are easy to defend.

scenarios.title: Attacking with our Defenders Supporting
scenarios.description: We all move down the field together - keeping space

frames.label: Attacking
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: Look at this large space between our midfielders and defenders, where the other team will win the ball.

frames.label: Attacking
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: Our defense needs to shift up to about midfield

frames.label: Attacking with our Defenders
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: If the ball gets cleared, our defense should put it back in play to continue our attack

title: 3. Through Ball
keyPhrase: Pass into space, run onto it.
summaryBullets: The runner should start when the passer is ready to see and play the ball.
The pass usually goes into space, not straight to feet.
Timed runs stretch the line without arriving too early.

scenarios.title: Through Ball
scenarios.description: The Forward waits for it, then breaking behind the defense at the right moment.
summary: Frame 1: the forward checks position and waits.
Frame 2: the pass is available and the run attacks the gap.

frames.label: Wait
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: Pass the ball into space behind the defense. Outside forwards need to be wide to create gaps

frames.label: Wait for kick
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: Outside forward waits till ball is kicked (to stay onside)

frames.label: Support
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

title: 4. Crossing the ball
keyPhrase: Attack different spaces in the box.  Don't overrun the ball
summaryBullets: Not every runner should attack the same spot.
One runner can go near post, one central, one far post, and another stay for a cutback.
Someone should still stay behind the attack for safety.

scenarios.title: One Wide Ball, Multiple Runs
scenarios.description: Attacking from wide. Box runs spread across different zones.

frames.label: Attacking Cross
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: Forwards, don't get too far ahead.  It's easier to run onto the ball then to come back for it.

frames.label: Attacking Cross
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: If you are too close, the goalie will intercept the cross

frames.label: Score

overlay.text: When the ball crosses. Run onto it. And score a goal!

frames.label: Score!
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.

overlay.text: When the ball crosses. Run onto it. And score a goal!

title: 5. Clearing the ball
summaryBullets: The whole shape should shift with the ball, not just the nearest player.
The far side stays connected instead of getting stranded on the touchline.
The team should be compact enough to support the play together.

scenarios.title: Clear the Ball
scenarios.description: Don't overplay the ball in this danger zone. Just clear it!

frames.label: Clear Away Zone
notes: Explain what the team shape looks like in this frame.
Call out the key movement or spacing idea.

overlay.text: If under attacking pressure, any ball coming past this imaginary line should be cleared away, down the side lines.

title: 6. When We Attack, The Defense Comes With Us
keyPhrase: Step up when there is pressure.
summaryBullets: The back line should come higher when the attack is established.
The sweeper and goalkeeper stay connected behind the line.
A compact team can win second balls and stop easy counterattacks.

scenarios.title: Deep Back Line vs Stepped Up
scenarios.description: Compare a stretched team to a connected team with the defenders up behind the midfield.
summary: The first frame leaves too much space between the lines.
The second frame compresses the team so it can attack and defend together.
